2606.23611.title: Data Selection Through Iterative Self-Filtering for Vision-Language Settings
2606.23611.paper_summary: The availability of large amounts of clean data is paramount to training neural networks. However, at large scales, manual oversight is impractical, resulting in sizeable datasets that can be very noisy. Attempts to mitigate this obstacle to producing performant vision-language models have so far involved heuristics, curated reference datasets, and using pre-trained models. Here we propose a novel, bootstrapped method in which a CLIP model is trained on an evolving, self-selected dataset. This evolving dataset constitutes a balance of filtered, highly probable clean samples as well as diverse samples from the entire distribution. Our proposed Self-Filtering method iterates between training the model and selecting a subsequently improved data mixture. Training on vision-language datasets filtered by the proposed approach improves downstream performance without the need for additional data or pre-trained models.
2606.23611.paper_summary_zh: 大量乾淨數據的可用性對於訓練神經網絡至關重要。<lb/>然而，在大規模的情況下，手動監督是不切實際的，導致生成的數據集可能非常嘈雜。<lb/>迄今為止，為了減輕這一障礙以產生高效的視覺-語言模型，所採取的措施包括啟發式方法、策劃的參考數據集以及使用預訓練模型。<lb/>在此，我們提出了一種新穎的自啟發方法，其中 CLIP 模型在一個不斷演變的自選數據集上進行訓練。<lb/>這個不斷演變的數據集包含了過濾後的高概率乾淨樣本與來自整個分佈的多樣樣本之間的平衡。<lb/>我們提出的自我過濾方法在訓練模型和選擇隨後改進的數據混合之間進行迭代。<lb/>在通過所提方法過濾的視覺-語言數據集上訓練，能夠提高下游性能，而無需額外的數據或預訓練模型。
2606.23611.author: Andrei Liviu Nicolicioiu et.al.
2606.23611.authors: Andrei Liviu Nicolicioiu, Sarvjeet Singh Ghotra, Morgane M. Moss, Aaron Courville
